negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing strict scheduler memory rebuild. The port can scan active Scheduler storage and uses saturating max-block helpers, but it does not hydrate/rebuild a scheduler-memory map, preserve origin_hash in that memory, reject duplicate call IDs during hydration, or set scheduler last_synced_block.
related_but_insufficient_code: compute_obligations/read_active_scheduled_calls read current storage directly, which reduces stale-memory risk but is not the startup hydration invariant.

rationale: The strict scan and memory replacement candidates form one current invariant: the scheduler must reconstruct memory from active on-chain storage instead of incrementally merging stale state.
statement: Startup hydration scans Scheduler slots, ignores empty slots, includes only decoded SCHEDULED calls, rejects duplicate call IDs or undecodable call mappings, preserves origin_hash, computes max_block_number with shared saturating math, replaces stale in-memory calls, and sets last_synced_block to the hydration block.
suggested_falsification_tests: Hydrate from storage containing empty slots, duplicate call IDs, undecodable call mappings, and origin_hash fields; assert exact memory replacement and last_synced_block update.
summary: Strict scheduler-memory rebuild hydration is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: No local scheduler-memory hydration surface exists; only parent-state scans are present.
title: Hydration strictly rebuilds memory from active Scheduler storage
